data.task:
ATOM 0 — Owner map first (DOCUMENTATION ONLY, no code/test changes). Extend docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md with the new 'Deb watcher' contract row(s) that this priority ('Deb follows Oscar - first-class tier-2 watcher') will build, so the owner map is the design-of-record before any runtime change.

WHAT TO ADD: In docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md, add a new dated subsection (e.g. '## Run_184 — Deb Watcher Contract') containing a table row (or small set of rows) that documents the runner-owned active Deb watch mechanism, with these columns matching the existing owner-map convention (Contract | Rightful single owner | Surfaces that re-encode/override | State and evidence) OR the later (Source of truth | Live emitters/consumers | Allowed write path | Test/proof) shape — pick whichever table you are appending near and stay consistent with it. The row(s) MUST capture:
  - Source of truth: the runner reuses the existing monitor primitive runMonitor (packages/core/src/runner/monitor.ts) and Deb status projection (packages/core/src/runner/status.ts) — NOT a new persona-polling loop. Name the intended runner owner module (packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts, alongside awaitOscarWithNudgeWatchdog) where the Deb watch is driven.
  - Live emitters/consumers: the events the watcher emits (store events) and the consumers — Deb's prompt language (packages/core/src/runner/prompts.ts), deb-status.json/deb-status.md projection (status.ts), and the Oscar-only nudge channel.
  - Write/nudge path: deb-nudge.json is the SINGLE nudge path with target fixed to 'oscar'; document the Oscar-requested Deb investigation/repair escalation path as the existing triage/repair owner (do NOT invent a second channel).
  - Test/proof: name the existing tests that will pin it (packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts, monitor.test.ts, status.test.ts, orchestration-contracts.test.ts) — these will be EXTENDED in atom 4, not replaced.

SINGLE-OWNER DISCIPLINE (critical): Do NOT duplicate the existing rows. The map already owns 'Deb live status projection' (the passive status feed), 'Execution path: Deb repair', 'COMMIT Deb repair', and 'REPORT run status to Deb'. Your new row is specifically the ACTIVE, event/transition-driven WATCH LOOP that keeps Deb informed across the FULL run lifecycle (directive wait, Bob build, verify wait, wrap, fault) — distinct from the passive projection. Reference the existing rows by their line/section instead of restating them. Explicitly state in prose that NO second watcher prompt, polling instruction, or parallel guide is being created — the watcher reuses runMonitor + the existing status/nudge owners.

State value: mark the new contract's current State as 'drifting' (the status projection exists but the active watch loop is not yet wired) and name the target owner that atoms 1-2 will implement.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
  - Only docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md is modified. No changes under packages/** or tests/**.
  - The new section is internally consistent with the surrounding table shape and cites real files/symbols that exist today (verify paths/symbols by opening the files).
  - No existing owner row is duplicated; existing rows are referenced, not restated.
  - Run the doc-pinning suite to prove no regression: `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/core test -- orchestration-contracts` (and report exit code + output). The owner-map doc is behavior-pinned; a content change must leave that suite green.

MUST NOT BREAK: the orchestration-contracts test suite; do not touch routing-guide taxonomy or any other doc section. Report the exact diff and the test command output (exit code) when done.

data.reason: Doc-only atom 0 exceeded scope with ~123 lines of untested runtime (directive.ts deb-investigate kind + runner.ts Deb watcher/escalation), and it regresses an existing test: runner.test.ts 'tracks Oz and Deb nudge seqs independently' now fails (Deb/Oz nudge ordering changed).

data.task:
ATOM 0 (re-scoped after rejection) — Owner map first. STRICTLY DOCUMENTATION ONLY.

HARD SCOPE GUARD (the prior attempt was rejected for violating this): You may modify EXACTLY ONE file: docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md. Do NOT touch anything under packages/** (no directive.ts, no runner.ts, no prompts.ts, no tests). Do NOT add a `deb-investigate` directive kind, a Deb watcher loop, or any escalation/fault code. This atom produces ZERO runtime/test changes. If you find yourself editing a .ts file, STOP — that is a different atom.

WHAT TO ADD: In docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md, append a new dated subsection '## Run_184 - Deb Watcher Contract (2026-06-22)' documenting, as the design-of-record, the runner-owned active Deb watch mechanism this priority will build. Match the table shape of the section you append near (Source of truth | Live emitters / consumers | Allowed write path | Test / proof). The row(s) MUST capture:
  - Source of truth: the runner will REUSE the existing monitor primitive runMonitor (packages/core/src/runner/monitor.ts) and the Deb status projection renderDebStatus (packages/core/src/runner/status.ts) — NOT a new persona-polling loop. Name the intended runner owner (packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts, alongside awaitOscarWithNudgeWatchdog) where atoms 1-2 will wire the watch.
  - Live emitters/consumers: the store events the watcher will emit and the consumers — Deb's prompt language (packages/core/src/runner/prompts.ts), deb-status.json/deb-status.md (status.ts), and the Oscar-only nudge channel.
  - Write/nudge path: deb-nudge.json is the SINGLE nudge path with target fixed to 'oscar'.
  - ESCALATION PATH — describe it as routing through the EXISTING Deb triage/repair owner (the 'Execution path: Deb repair' / 'COMMIT Deb repair' rows; packages/personas/base/deb.md + runner triage dispatch). Do NOT assert any specific new mechanism (e.g. a directive kind or a run-fail). The concrete escalation shape is an OPEN design question for atom 1; note it as 'design TBD in atom 1, must reuse the existing triage/repair owner, not a second channel'.
  - Test/proof: name the existing tests that will pin it and be EXTENDED (not replaced) in atom 4: packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts, monitor.test.ts, status.test.ts, orchestration-contracts.test.ts.

SINGLE-OWNER DISCIPLINE: Do NOT duplicate existing rows. The map already owns 'Deb live status projection' (passive feed), 'Execution path: Deb repair', 'COMMIT Deb repair', and 'REPORT run status to Deb' — reference them by section, do not restate them. Your new row is specifically the ACTIVE, event/transition-driven WATCH LOOP across the full lifecycle (directive wait, Bob build, verify wait, wrap, fault), distinct from the passive projection. State in prose that NO second watcher prompt, polling instruction, or parallel guide is being created. Mark current State as 'drifting' (status projection exists; active watch loop not yet wired).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
  - `git diff --name-only` shows ONLY docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md changed. Any packages/** change is an automatic fail.
  - Cited files/symbols exist today (open them to confirm paths/line refs).
  - Run the doc-pinning suite and report exit code + tail output: `pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' --filter @cocoder/core test -- orchestration-contracts`. It must stay green.

Report the diff and the test command output (exit code) when done.

data.reason: Diff is green but overruns the doc-only atom into the whole feature, and its escalation primitive (deb-investigate -> fail() -> run marked 'failed') makes Oscar's request for Deb FORMALLY FAIL the run, diverging from the Objective's 'investigate/repair to continue' intent — a hard-to-reverse spine+base-persona contract needing founder sign-off before commit.

data.outcome: ✅ COMMITTED on `main` — 2 commit(s) on the active branch (no landing step; work is on the branch by construction). Committed out-of-lane (flagged, NOT held back): cocoder/tickets/INDEX.md, cocoder/tickets/open/0030-deb-escalation-fork-fault-vs-continue.md.
